acceptance_targets: [chapter_01_F04] PASS if at least 3 of the cited lines are rewritten without the "not X, but/instead Y" pivot and each replacement adds concrete forward motion or sensory specificity; FAIL if the same negation template still recurs 3+ times across those targeted beats.

[chapter_01_F05] PASS if no more than 2 "as if" constructions remain in the revised equivalents of the cited lines and replacements maintain or increase scene specificity; FAIL if "as if" remains a repeated default across 3+ of those target spots.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least 3 of the cited lines are rewritten without the "not X, but/instead Y" pivot and each replacement adds concrete forward motion or sensory specificity; FAIL if the same negation template still recurs 3+ times across those targeted beats.
findings.problem: Recurring negation-correction construction appears well above the 2x threshold (e.g., "not X ..." then corrective clause), becoming a detectable sentence tic that flattens prose variety.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:165, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:187, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:215, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:325, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:349, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:427, replace at least 3 negation-pivot lines with direct affirmative observation, action, or sensation to diversify rhythm.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than 2 "as if" constructions remain in the revised equivalents of the cited lines and replacements maintain or increase scene specificity; FAIL if "as if" remains a repeated default across 3+ of those target spots.
findings.problem: The chapter overuses the "as if" interpretive template (7+ instances), which creates repetitive rhetorical hedging and weakens close-third immediacy.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the repeated "as if" constructions at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:293, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:333, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:361, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:403, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:429 into more varied sentence shapes grounded in direct perception or decisive metaphor.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when stakes demand it: show coercion mechanics, sexual negotiation, grief rituals, and financial desperation at scene level. In edge cases, prefer precision over decorum; protect character dignity through perspective accuracy, not by omitting the hard material.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene requires bodily, economic, or moral consequence and the draft retreats into abstract phrasing, rewrite with concrete action, sensation, and decision impact before proceeding.
aesthetic_risk_policy.operational_resolution_rule: When uncertain between tasteful distance and narrative truth, choose the version that preserves causal accountability and embodied consequence, then tune language for clarity rather than softness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, legal language, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter structures deliberately: high-density dialogue vs interior reflection, multi-scene public collision vs two-scene intimate pressure, aftermath inventory vs anticipatory dread. Never let two consecutive chapters share the same pacing architecture or turn mechanism.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsentimental literary diction that permits technical vocabulary, profanity, and class-marked speech without flattening idiolects.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person centered on Seo Ina, with brief controlled widenings in public-event chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternating pressure waves: compressed clauses in operational stress, longer breath-based lines in grief and reflection.
